preview_text:
The model favours Spain at 48%, with France rated at just 22% to win. This represents a substantial gap; Spain is judged more than twice as likely to take the match. France's underdog position is marked, though the draw probability at 30% suggests competitive equilibrium over ninety minutes remains plausible.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects Spain's clear advantage but also the pronounced role of stalemate. A draw is nearly as probable as a Spanish victory, indicating the teams are expected to be closely matched tactically despite the model's lean toward Spain. The gap between Spain's win probability and France's suggests an asymmetric contest, yet draws remain the single most likely outcome under the model.

The model and sportsbooks diverge markedly. Bookmakers and Polymarket assign France roughly 38–38% to win and Spain 31–30%, inverting the model's assessment almost entirely. The gap between model and consensus for France exceeds 15 percentage points, with Spain showing a comparable inversion. No clear alignment exists here; the dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more reliable.
